title: Daily Market Digest: June 19, 2026
subtitle: Market closes for Juneteenth holiday after a tech-led rally

items.headline: US Markets Closed for Juneteenth; Thursday Saw Tech-Led Gains
items.summary: U.S. equity markets are closed today, June 19, 2026, in observance of the Juneteenth holiday. Trading activity on Thursday was largely positive, with technology stocks driving significant gains across major indices. Investor sentiment was bolstered by the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and easing global energy supply concerns, although the Federal Reserve's recent hawkish stance on future interest rates continues to be a key consideration. Early Friday futures indicated mixed movements before the holiday closure.

items.headline: Intel Surges on Apple Chip Deal
items.summary: Intel Corporation saw a significant jump after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the semiconductor giant would manufacture chips for Apple in the U.S.

items.headline: Bloom Energy Jumps on Data Center News
items.summary: Bloom Energy ($BE) rose sharply after UBS highlighted a positive decision by FERC regarding data center power.

items.headline: Nvidia Extends Gains on AI Strength
items.summary: Nvidia continued its strong performance, driven by optimism around AI memory solutions.

items.headline: Accenture Falls on Weak Outlook
items.summary: Accenture plc experienced a steep decline after cutting its revenue outlook and receiving downgrades due to concerns about weakening demand for IT consulting in an AI-driven market.

items.headline: Kroger Down After Profit Guidance
items.summary: Kroger Co. shares fell following the release of weaker-than-expected profit guidance.

items.headline: SpaceX Continues Post-Debut Slide
items.summary: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. ($SPCX) continued its decline for the second consecutive day since its recent stock market debut.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amidst Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained its key interest rate this week. However, the latest projections indicate that more officials anticipate at least one rate hike by the end of 2026, suggesting that borrowing costs could remain elevated for a longer period.

items.headline: Strong Consumer Spending Evident in Latest Economic Data
items.summary: Recent economic data showed resilience in consumer activity, with US retail sales increasing by 0.9% in May and pending home sales climbing by 3.8% in the same month. Philadelphia factory activity also rebounded in June.

items.headline: Geopolitical Developments Ease Oil Prices, Boost Sentiment
items.summary: The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, following a US-Iran agreement to end their conflict, has eased global energy supply concerns. This development contributed to lower oil prices and improved overall risk sentiment in the markets.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Market Rally; IT Consulting Faces AI Scrutiny
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly semiconductor companies, was a leading force in Thursday's market rally. However, concerns about IT consulting demand in an AI-dominated world impacted companies like Accenture and Cognizant, highlighting potential sector rotations.

items.headline: US Dollar Index Reaches One-Year High
items.summary: The U.S. dollar effective exchange rate against other developed currencies reached a one-year high on Friday, continuing its strengthening trend.
